mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and supports risk appetite, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts occur after growth deterioration and equities continue to fall despite easing.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS declines on a 3-month basis, or if walk-forward IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and lower expected household income, which reduces equity valuations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises because of supply-side participation effects without broad demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad demand strength in payroll income and retail spending, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails when retail sales rise only from inflation, not real demand.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-change condition on PAYEMS and RSAFS does not beat either series alone in out-of-sample classification, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive more than 55% of the time when both proxies improve.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and supply-side utilization, which signals stronger cyclical activity and tends to produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if production rises only because inventories are being rebuilt while demand stays weak.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises on a 2-month average basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO trigger periods do not deliver higher 20-day returns than non-trigger periods, or if the sign hit rate falls below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays low, real policy rates fall and liquidity conditions ease, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if inflation spikes are interpreted as stagflation and equities sell off instead.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS do not outperform the unconditional 60-day return distribution, or if forward returns are negative in more than half of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below its recent trend signals weakening housing demand and construction activity, which leads investors to mark down cyclical earnings and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if housing starts fall while other demand measures are still expanding strongly.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the average forward return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A combined rise in UNRATE and decline in PAYEMS identifies genuine labor-market stress, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if unemployment rises without payroll contraction and equities remain resilient.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-up/PAYEMS-down condition does not produce lower 5-day returns than either series alone, or if the hit rate is below 55% over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession rebound, simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals that both output and consumer demand are reaccelerating, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails outside recovery phases because GDP gains can be inventory-led and not equity-positive.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and RSAFS improvement does not outperform unconditional 60-day returns in recovery periods, or if positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered cases.
